tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh RC=0 with 'Phase gates consistent.'

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D regression confirmation: no script auto-advances .current_phase after gate passes; had to manually set jq '.current_phase = 2 | .gates.phase_0_to_1 / phase_1_to_2 = date'. Recorded as Medium prior-sweep bug.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all phase1_architecture steps and running scripts/check-phase-gate.sh (RC=0), .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase remains 0. No documented script in scripts/ updates .current_phase from gate-pass evidence.
framework_bugs.expected: A scripted action should advance .current_phase from 0->1 once all phase1_architecture steps and the 0->1 gate evidence are present (and similarly 1->2, 2->3).
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase stays at the previous value indefinitely; only manual jq edits or upgrade-project.sh in special modes change it.

documentation_gaps.gap: No documented procedure (script or doc) for advancing .current_phase after a gate passes — agents are silently expected to either edit phase-state.json manually or rely on out-of-band logic.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: .build_loop.feature='uat-feat-1' after --start-feature.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build_loop steps (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit, documentation_updated) completed for both uat-feat-1 and uat-feat-2 sequentially. Stub artifacts: tests/uat-feat-N.test.swift, src/uat-feat-N.swift, docs/security-audits/uat-feat-N-security-audit.md (scan stubbed for UAT).

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded RC=0 after all 5 build_loop steps; commit hook emitted only an informational warning about test ordering, did not block.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: Simulated PreToolUse payload {tool_input.command:'git commit -m "feat(...)"'} fed to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh produced permissionDecision=deny with the expected BL-006 message. PR #17 hook payload fix (.tool_input.command) verified.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 and --start-feature uat-feat-2 + 5 build_loop steps, simulated PreToolUse payload for feat(uat-2): commit was permitted by gate (no deny output). The build_loop reset on feature_recorded works as designed (process-checklist.sh:464).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: After scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer with valid schema, simulated commit payload was denied with reason 'pre-commit gate: commit blocked — pending user decision.' BL-015 sentinel honored.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent simulated chore: commit payload received no deny (RC=0).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .features_completed contains both 'uat-feat-1' and 'uat-feat-2'; features_since_last_test=2.

tests.name: u_c_direct_shell_commit_bypass
tests.details: U-C confirmation: 'git commit --allow-empty -m "feat(bl006-trigger): no loop"' executed directly from the shell succeeded with RC=0 — there is no git commit-msg hook installed by init.sh, so direct shell commits bypass BL-006 entirely. (Only PreToolUse-routed commits are gated.)

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct git commit bypasses BL-006 (no commit-msg hook)
framework_bugs.reproduction: From $PROJECT_DIR with no active build_loop: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop' — RC=0, commit lands. Repository has no .git/hooks/commit-msg hook installed by init.sh.
framework_bugs.expected: init.sh should install a git commit-msg hook (or pre-commit hook) that invokes pre-commit-gate.sh / a BL-006 check, so direct shell commits are also gated, not just PreToolUse-routed ones.
framework_bugs.actual: Only the PreToolUse Claude-Code-mediated path enforces BL-006; direct shell git commits with feat: subject succeed even with no Build Loop active.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched through gate_passed) completed sequentially with RC=0. No artifact gates on uat_session steps.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh RC=0; phase 4 is intentionally blocked at this gate for sponsored_poc deployments per design ('Phase 4 is BLOCKED — project is in sponsored poc mode'). Several Phase 3->4 warnings (HANDOFF.md, sbom.json, docs/test-results/, SECURITY.md, pen test) are expected for a stub project — these would be authored in a real workflow.

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires scenario.steps to be a string, not a JSON array, but the templates/uat/test-session-template.html documentation (around line 148) implies arrays are acceptable. The linter does `head -1` on the jq-extracted value, which yields '[' for arrays and triggers the state-restatement check failure. Author scenarios with steps as a single "\n"-joined string.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh:113-129 (state-restatement check) and templates/uat/test-session-template.html scenario schema comment

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 emits '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in sponsored poc mode' and exits with RC=0 (informational warning, not error). This is intentional per the sponsored_poc gov-mode design — Phase 4 is unlocked only via scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production.

summary: sponsored_poc/mobile/standard/swift base agent: init.sh --non-interactive (PR #19) succeeded; all PR #17/#18 fixes verified (BL-015 sentinel, framework-self guard, U-H helper-copy, BL-006 grace window via PreToolUse, hook payload .tool_input.command); confirmed two still-open prior-sweep bugs at Medium severity (U-C direct-shell commit bypass, U-D no auto-phase-advancement); no Critical or High findings; no framework contamination.
